items.title: 'Completely unimaginable': Man critically injured in robot vacuum fire

children.content: The family of a Perth man critically injured when a robot vacuum caught fire at his home say their lives have been "turned upside down" by the "horrific" incident.

items.title: Does Alcoa's $8b acquisition leave WA's jarrah forests in the firing line?

image.altText: Kingsley Dixon sits on a hill of orange brown rubble, in front of a horizon of mined land.

children.content: Alcoa's multi-billion-dollar deal to buy South32's alumina operations could unlock swathes of Western Australia's native jarrah forests for bauxite mining, environmentalists say.

items.title: Widow not told of husband's death on FIFO swing until 14 hours later

children.content: A Perth widow says she has been kept in the dark about the circumstances leading to the death of her husband as he worked on an offshore gas plant more than 18 months ago.

items.title: Discounts 'too little, too late' after slow start to WA's northern tourist season

children.content: The WA government says a discounted tours scheme will help revitalise the Kimberley's sluggish start to its annual tourism season but operators have questioned the voucher program.

image.altText: A girl stands holding a suitcase at the end of the groyne at Town Beach in Broome looking out to the ocean

items.title: AFL legend Tony Modra 'overwhelmed by the kindness' after truck crash

children.content: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: Labor in 'fight for our lives' as by-election opens door to One Nation assault

children.content: Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons sparks a by-election in the WA minister and Labor stalwart's southern Perth seat at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

articles.title: By-election to follow minister's resignation amid One Nation surge

children.content: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

items.title: 'Disgusting' antisemitic graffiti sprayed on Anzac landmark

children.content: Swastikas and anti-Jewish abuse were discovered spray-painted on rocks and the historic Plantagenet Battery along Western Australia's south coast.

items.title: Australia urged to sign treaty banning nuclear weapons

children.content: There are renewed calls for Australia to sign a treaty ensuring nuclear weapons can't ever be facilitated, stored or manufactured in Australia.

image.altText: An orange flash had been enveloped by the great uprush of water. Immense clouds of smoke, steam and spray burst into the air.

items.title: Visa woes behind him, Bryce Cotton stars for Boomers on return to Perth

children.content: Six-time NBL MVP Bryce Cotton shines as he pulls on Australian colours for the first time in the Boomers' FIBA World Cup qualifiers.

image.altText: Bryce Cotton shoots against the Philippines in a FIBA World Cup qualifier, arms outstretched with the ball going for goal.

items.title: Swimmer who disappeared from Perth beach 13 years ago likely killed by shark

children.content: Martyn Tann was last seen at Mullaloo Beach in 2013, with a coroner telling an inquest into his disappearance that she believed he was dead, likely in a shark attack.

items.title: Microsoft workers in Australia to be caught up in 4,800 global job cuts

children.content: ABC News understands Microsoft Australia staff will be impacted by the tech giant's latest round of lay-offs. Microsoft will cut about 2.1 per cent of its global workforce.

items.title: Sick of paying for streaming services? These films and TV series are free

children.content: From Bluey in Yolŋu Matha to the first feature film directed by an Indigenous woman, there is a trove of free movies and TV to dive into this NAIDOC Week.

items.title: A frame-by-frame look at Balogun's infamous red card tackle

children.content: While many agree the red card call was too harsh, the story has become a bigger discussion about FIFA's integrity.

articles.title: 'Not his fault': Belgium coach consoles Balogun after World Cup controversy

children.content: Belgium did not feel it was fair Folarin Balogun played for the US in the round of 16 match after he was given a red card in his team's previous match.

image.altText: Folarin Balogun is consoled by Rudi Garcia.

articles.title: Quick hits: American dream comes crashing down, Mbappé hits back at abuse

children.content: The US goes crashing out of its own World Cup after days of controversy and rancour, while Kylian Mbappé refuses to stand for dreadful racist abuse. Here are today's quick hits.

items.title: What you need to know before claiming your phone as a tax deduction

children.content: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Your challenge in today's ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

items.title: Richard White resigns as WiseTech chair but will remain with firm

children.content: The billionaire founder of WiseTech Global said he was stepping down as the company's chair after "recent personal media attention" created an "unnecessary distraction" from the business.

image.altText: Close up of Richard White smiling on stage.

items.title: Boys major victims of AI-assisted online sexual abuse, study finds

children.content: About one in every 25 Australians under the age of 18 has been the victim or knows a victim of artificial intelligence-assisted online sexual abuse, according to a new study.

image.altText: A maths student studies with a calculator on their desk.

items.title: Declining vaccination rates behind whooping cough spike across WA's south

children.content: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already surged to more than three times last year's numbers, with parents urged to get their children vaccinated.

items.title: Families told accused Bali bomber Hambali to face 202 life terms

children.content: US military prosecutors reveal new details about their case against the man accused of planning the 2002 Bali bombings, telling the loved ones of Australian victims his trial could finally begin next year.

image.altText: A bespectacled middle aged Indonesian man with a grey beard but no moustache wears a black and white top.

items.title: International experts weigh in on Australia's bird flu response

children.content: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

image.altText: Black swans at swan river in Perth.

items.title: US toddler found in pool and declared dead later found alive

children.content: Five hours after the young boy was pronounced dead next to a suburban swimming pool in the US state of Arizona, police were notified that the child was breathing again at a local hospital.

items.title: 'Not about race': Winmar statue removed two days after assault conviction

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.
